_provenance: schema-derived from R1 AC: trip, dismiss, re-trip, auto-clear, daemon-restart semantics. ISO timestamps use fixed wall-clock values matching existing fleet_correlation fixture convention. No producer-computed values.
description: Lifecycle scenarios for fleet_alert_state table: initial trip, dismiss, re-trip, auto-clear

cases.label: initial_trip_writes_to_fleet_alert_state
cases.note: Engine detects fleet event; write_fleet_alert() stores it with dismissed_at_et=NULL

cases.label: dismiss_sets_dismissed_at_et_non_null
cases.note: Operator dismisses; dismissed_at_et is set to a non-NULL ISO string; other fields unchanged

cases.label: re_trip_after_dismiss_nulls_dismissed_at_et
cases.note: New fleet event in same cycle after dismiss: write_fleet_alert() with new tripped_at_et and dismissed_at_et=NULL makes alert visible again

cases.label: auto_clear_removes_row
cases.note: Auto-clear timer calls clear_fleet_alert(); row is deleted; read_fleet_alert() returns None

cases.label: daemon_restart_alert_persists
cases.note: Fleet alert written before restart; after daemon restart (new DB connection) read_fleet_alert() returns the same row
